mnemonics: Create A New Folder, Reach Into
keyPoints: The mkdir function in Unix creates a new directory.
The mkdir function requires a directory path as an argument.
The mkdir function creates the directory and its parents if they do not exist.
The rmdir function in Unix removes a directory and all its contents.
The rmdir function requires a directory path as an argument.
The rmdir function will only remove the directory if it is empty.
